name: Mary McLeod Bethune
description: American educator and civil rights leader (1875-1955)
abstract: Mary McLeod Bethune (née McLeod; July 10, 1875 – May 18, 1955) was an American educator, philanthropist, humanitarian, womanist, and civil rights activist. Bethune founded the National Council of Negro Women in 1935, and proceeded to establish the Aframerican Women's Journal, which was the flagship journal of the organization.

about_quotes.evidence: I had a chance to meet Mrs. Bethune, because she did some of the baccalaureate services there.
